output.formatted:
Fox Broadcasting Company

SPARQL query over wikidata:
```sparql
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
SELECT ?network WHERE {
  wd:Q729265 wdt:P449 ?network .
}
```

messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
Which tv network originally aired the science fiction tv series Space: Above and Beyond

content.message:
To determine which TV network originally aired the science fiction TV series *Space: Above and Beyond*, I need to:

1. Identify the TV series *Space: Above and Beyond* in Wikidata.
2. Search for the property that specifies the "originally aired on" or "broadcast network" of the series.
3. Retrieve the TV network associated with this property.

### Step 1: Search for the entity "Space: Above and Beyond"
I will first search for the entity "Space: Above and Beyond" in Wikidata to confirm its existence and get its identifier.

tool_calls.name: search_entity
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 entity alternatives:
1. Space: Above and Beyond (wd:Q729265), also known as "Silicate":
  - American science fiction TV series
  - is instance of television series
2. list of Space: Above and Beyond episodes (wd:Q6597738):
  - is instance of Wikimedia list article
  - Wikimedia list article
3. Category:Space: Above and Beyond episode redirects to lists (wd:Q121597813):
  - is instance of Wikimedia category of redirects
  - Wikimedia category
4. Race to space and beyond (wd:Q129968705):
  - is instance of scholarly article
  - scholarly article published in March 2018
5. Above & Beyond (wd:Q323187, matched via "Above and Beyond"), also known as "Above and Beyond":

content.message:
The top result for "Space: Above and Beyond" is **(wd:Q729265)**, which is correctly identified as an American science fiction TV series. This is the relevant entity we will use.

### Step 2: Search for properties related to "aired on" or "broadcast network"
Now, I will search for properties in Wikidata related to "aired on" or "broadcast network" to find the correct property that links a TV series to its original network.

I will search for properties related to "airing" or "network".

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. original broadcaster (wd:P449 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "aired on"), also known as "aired on", "airs on", "broadcast on", "channel", "first air channel", etc.:
  - network(s) or service(s) that originally broadcast a radio or television program
2. broadcast by (wd:P3301 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "aired on"), also known as "aired on", "broadcasted on", "broadcaster", "broadcasting on", "broadcast on", etc.:
  - channel, network, website or service that broadcast this item over radio, television or the Internet
3. located on street (wd:P669 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "is on"), also known as "address street", "is on", "located at street (item)", "on street", "road", etc.:
  - street, road, square, or trail, where the item is located. To ... P6375 "street address", if there is no item for the street
4. connecting line (wd:P81 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "is on"), also known as "is on", "line connected to", "rail line connected to", "railway line":
  - railway line(s) subject is directly connected to
5. located in/on physical feature (wd:P706 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "is on"), also known as "geographical region", "is in", "is on", "landform", "located on island", etc.:
  - located on the specified (geo)physical feature. Should not be ... range (P4552). Use P206 for things in/on bodies of water.
6. inception (wd:P571 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "installated on"), also known as "built", "commenced on date", "commencement date", "completed", "composed", etc.:
  - time when an entity begins to exist; for date of official opening use P1619
7. separated from (wd:P807 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "spun off"), also known as "branched from", "broke from", "broke off from", "forked from", "schism from", etc.:
  - subject was founded or started by separating from identified object
8. point in time (wd:P585 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "on"), also known as "as of", "at time", "by date", "date", "during", etc.:
  - date something took place, existed or a statement was true; for providing time use the "refine date" property (P4241)
9. audio system (wd:P7501 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "audio format":
  - audio system hardware used in the item
10. carries passengers or cargo (wd:P3437 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "onboard"), also known as "brings", "cargo carried", "cargo transported", "carries", "carries cargo", etc.:
  - type of passengers or cargo a vehicle, transport network, or operator carries

content.message:
The most relevant property for identifying the original broadcasting network of a TV series is **"original broadcaster" (wdt:P449)**, which matches "aired on", "broadcast on", and "channel". This property directly links a TV series to the network that originally aired it.

### Step 3: Retrieve the original broadcaster of *Space: Above and Beyond*
Now, I will retrieve the value of the "original broadcaster" property for the TV series *Space: Above and Beyond* (wd:Q729265) using a SPARQL query.

Final SPARQL query:
```sparql
SELECT ?network
WHERE {
  wd:Q729265 wdt:P449 ?network .
}
```

I will now execute this query to get the original TV network.

tool_calls.name: execute
tool_calls.result:
Got 1 row and 1 column:
| network                               |
| ------------------------------------- |
| Fox Broadcasting Company (wd:Q166419) |

content.message:
The TV network that originally aired the science fiction TV series *Space: Above and Beyond* is the **Fox Broadcasting Company**.

Final answer:
